messages.author: LittleWan
messages.text: Hi, I am an embedded IoT engineer. I am currently working on some research projects where i am exploring alternative communication protocols, based on LoRa. I developed LoRaWAN sensors in the past, and I would like to validate the value of MeshCore for IoT sensor applications. The homepage mentions 'IoT sensor networks' but have not yet seen any implementations of it... My goals is to test what the capabilities are with MeshCore. I have a Sensecap T1000-E that has a GPS sensor. I flashed the USB companion firmware and am configuring everything using the meshcli software. I would like to create/configure a tracker that publishes its location to a private channel at a regular interval. I am struggling a bit at the moment. Does anyone have some tips on how to implement this? 1) Is it possible to send sensor values (telemetry) to a channel? 2) Polling for telemetry is possible, but can i also Publish at a timed interval? This means my tracker does not have te listen, en this has a huge impact on the batterylife. If someone has any examples of pure 'IoT sensor networks' applications, i would like to know about it. Thank you

Thank you

messages.author: Nemo 🇨🇭
messages.text: Hi, I'm using myself T1000-A (on Helium LoRaWAN), and T1000-E (MeshCore Companion) I didn't searched for the "publish" functionality you're looking for on your MeshCore device
messages.content_html: Hi, I'm using myself T1000-A (on Helium LoRaWAN), and T1000-E (MeshCore Companion) I didn't searched for the "publish" functionality you're looking for on your MeshCore device
messages.reply_html:
LittleWan
Hi, I am an embedded IoT engineer. I am currently working on some research projects where i am exploring alternative communication protocols, based on LoRa. I developed LoRaWAN sensors in the past, and I would like to validate the value of MeshCore for IoT sensor applications. The homepage mentions 'IoT sensor networks' but have not yet seen any implementations of it... My goals is to test what the capabilities are with MeshCore. I have a Sensecap T1000-E that has a GPS sensor. I flashed the USB companion firmware and am configuring everything using the meshcli software. I would like to create/configure a tracker that publishes its location to a private channel at a regular interval. I am struggling a bit at the moment. Does anyone have some tips on how to implement this? 1) Is it possible to send sensor values (telemetry) to a channel? 2) Polling for telemetry is possible, but can i also Publish at a timed interval? This means my tracker does not have te listen, en this has a huge impact on the batterylife. If someone has any examples of pure 'IoT sensor networks' applications, i would like to know about it. Thank you

messages.author: LittleWan
messages.text: LoRaWAN class A allows for very optimal battery usage. I designed sensors that can go for 10 years on a single AA battery and delivering data every 15 minutes... Now that was a different case. But i believe in sensors that can use the MeshCore network as a backbone. The flexible nature of small and portable nodes in MeshCore compared to the static, and rather large immobile LoRaWAN gateways enables other use-cases. Particular mobile use-cases such as gps tracking... I might dive into the code and try to implement it myself, but if possible i want to leverage as much as possible of the default features already available in the protocol.

messages.author: supernovasky
messages.content_html: @LittleWan big emphasis on this part
messages.reply_html:
Nemo 🇨🇭
Perhaps it's not possible to do interval publishing on MeshCore, because it does per design not flood the network with telemetry data
